ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Thai green prawn curry; closest_parent 'curry food product' (FOODON_00002539) used as the nearest defensible ancestor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Curry dish) is the closest available parent category; no specific Thai green prawn curry code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for Thai green prawn curry as a specific food entity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of Thai green prawn curry is approximately 350 g, comprising a generous portion of prawns in coconut-milk green curry sauce with vegetables, typically served alongside steamed jasmine rice.

nutrient_source.title: Thai Green Curry with Prawns — nutrient profile estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data: Crustaceans, shrimp, mixed species, cooked, moist heat (FDC ID 175180); Coconut milk, canned (FDC ID 169411); Green curry paste (FDC ID 169238); and FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table for Southeast Asia (2nd ed.) reference values for Thai curry dishes
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for Thai green prawn curry as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted-average composition of primary ingredients (approximately 30% prawns, 35% coconut milk, 15% vegetables and aromatics, 10% green curry paste, 10% cooking liquid/fish sauce) using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component entries and FAO/INFOODS SEA reference values for Thai curry dishes. Values are consistent with published Thai green curry nutrient ranges in peer-reviewed literature.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of coconut milk-based Thai curry dishes — analogous estimate based on Thai green curry (coconut milk base, low carbohydrate) referenced against University of Sydney GI Database entries for coconut milk curry and mixed seafood curry dishes; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Thai green prawn curry. GI estimated at 38 (low) based on analogous coconut milk-based Thai curry entries in the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2008 International Tables of GI values (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008). The low GI reflects the dominant fat and protein content from coconut milk and prawns, with minimal rapidly digestible carbohydrate. Glycemic load per 350 g serving calculated as (38 × 4.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 3.5) / 100 = approximately 6.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from fish sauce and green curry paste
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to reduce sodium intake, as a 350 g serving provides approximately 1470 mg sodium, representing over 60% of the recommended daily limit of 2000 mg; requesting reduced fish sauce or low-sodium curry paste is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index and low net carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Thai green prawn curry has a low glycemic index (estimated 38) and a low glycemic load per serving (approximately 6), making it a suitable choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without large quantities of steamed rice; the high protein and fat content from prawns and coconut milk further attenuates postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat from coconut milk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Coconut milk contributes approximately 5.9 g saturated fat per 100 g, which may raise LDL cholesterol with frequent consumption; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion frequency and consider reduced-coconut-milk preparations, while benefiting from the cardioprotective omega-3 fatty acids and lean protein provided by the prawns.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Thai Green Prawn Curry (แกงเขียวหวานกุ้ง, Kaeng Khiao Wan Kung) is a classic Thai seafood curry dish featuring succulent prawns simmered in a rich, aromatic coconut milk sauce made with green curry paste. The green curry paste is prepared from fresh green chillies, lemongrass, galangal, kaffir lime leaves, shallots, garlic, and shrimp paste, giving the dish its characteristic vibrant green colour and complex herbal flavour profile. The dish is finished with Thai basil, fish sauce, and palm sugar, and typically includes bamboo shoots and Thai eggplant. The flavour is simultaneously spicy, creamy, slightly sweet, and fragrant, with a silky coconut milk base that tempers the heat of the green chillies. Nutritionally, Thai green prawn curry is a moderate-energy dish (approximately 118 kcal per 100 g) with a good protein contribution from the prawns (approximately 8.2 g per 100 g) and significant fat from coconut milk (approximately 7.8 g per 100 g, predominantly saturated). The carbohydrate content is low (approximately 4.5 g per 100 g), resulting in a low glycemic index (estimated 38) and low glycemic load, making it suitable for individuals managing blood glucose. Sodium content is elevated due to fish sauce and curry paste, warranting caution for individuals with hypertension. The dish is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free. In Singapore, this dish is widely available at Thai restaurants and seafood-focused establishments such as Shrimp Prawn Seafood at Boat Quay, where it may be marketed under creative names such as 'Incredible Green Hulk Curry'. It is typically served with steamed jasmine rice.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Thai green prawn curry as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from weighted-average USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component data and FAO/INFOODS SEA reference values. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are high-quality approved sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. The commercial menu name 'Incredible Green Hulk Curry' is a restaurant-specific marketing name used by Shrimp Prawn Seafood at Boat Quay (field survey data). The canonical food name has been normalised to 'Thai Green Prawn Curry' for database purposes; the marketing name is preserved in aliases. 2. Nutrient values are estimated from component data; a direct laboratory analysis of this specific restaurant dish would improve confidence to high. 3. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central component sources used for this estimation. 4. Trans fat is reported as 0.0 g per 100 g; this is confirmed as effectively zero for a coconut-milk and prawn-based dish with no hydrogenated fat ingredients. 5. Sodium content (420 mg per 100 g) reflects typical fish sauce and curry paste usage; actual restaurant preparation may vary. 6. GI set to 38 (low) based on analogous coconut milk-based Thai curry entries from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008) Diabetes Care international GI tables. 7. Halal status is marked true as the dish contains no pork or alcohol by standard preparation; individual restaurant certification should be verified. 8. FoodOn match_type is closest_parent as no exact FoodOn term for Thai green prawn curry exists. FoodEx2 match_type is closest_parent as A0CKP (Curry dish) is the nearest available category.
